question: What are the components of the NuScale Power Module (NPM)?

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0061.title: Control Rod Drive Mechanism Proof Test
nuscale_ch01_0061.summary: The NuScale Integral System Test Program uses the NIST-1 facility, a scaled model of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), to generate thermal-hydraulic data for system characterization and validation of safety analysis codes such as NRELAP5 and PIM. These tests, conducted at Oregon State University, ensure that the unique features and safety methodologies of the NPM are accurately represented and validated for licensing and design certification purposes.

nuscale_ch01_0063.title: Emergency Core Cooling System Valve Design Certification Application Demonstration Test
nuscale_ch01_0063.summary: This section describes the demonstration test program for the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves in the NuScale reactor design. The test program aimed to verify the functional performance of the ECCS valves, including their operation at reactor pressures and temperatures and in borated coolant, as well as the effectiveness of the inadvertent actuation block (IAB) feature. Testing was conducted at a specialized facility using a test article representative of the reactor recirculation valve (RRV) to ensure the valves meet safety and operational requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and startup procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building and include spaces for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock, all designed to facilitate safe fuel handling and reactor maintenance. These areas provide equipment and access for refueling operations, new fuel receipt, inspection, and storage, while ensuring radiation protection for personnel.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies the use of representative conceptual design information (CDI), and notes that each COL applicant must reference and supplement the NuScale FSAR with site-specific details. Security-related information is specially marked and withheld according to regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0002.title: Introduction
nuscale_ch01_0002.summary: The Introduction section of the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) outlines the purpose and scope of the document, which is to provide the required safety analysis and design information for NuScale’s standard design certification application. It describes the modular NuScale Power Module (NPM), its key components, and the plant’s scalable design, highlighting unique safety features such as passive shutdown and cooling, compact containment, and advanced digital controls. The FSAR covers both individual module and multi-module plant configurations, focusing on safety and operational limits.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides (RGs) and standards related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4. It details whether each RG is applicable, not applicable, conforms, or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections for further information. The table provides a clear overview of compliance status for each regulatory criterion.

nuscale_ch01_0135.title: Table 1.9-3: Conformance with NUREG-0800, Standard Review Plan (SRP) and Design Specific Review Standard (DSRS)
nuscale_ch01_0135.summary: Table 1.9-3 summarizes NuScale’s conformance with the regulatory criteria outlined in NUREG-0800 (Standard Review Plan) and the Design Specific Review Standard. The table lists each SRP or DSRS section, describes the acceptance criteria, and indicates whether NuScale’s application conforms or if the criteria are not applicable, with brief comments explaining each determination.

edges.description: Section B describes a test program that uses a scaled representation of the NPM design, which is one of the plant features discussed in Section A, thus Section B provides supporting evidence for the design features outlined in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale Power Module's design and safety analysis, while Section B specifies particular components and systems (such as chiller equipment and instrument air system) housed within the Central Utility Building, thereby detailing elements that are part of the overall plant described in Section A.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its safety analysis, while Section B describes a test program that generates data to characterize and validate the NPM's systems, thus supporting the detailed understanding of its components.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale Power Module's design and its components, while Section B gives specific details about one such component—the Emergency Core Cooling System valves—thus Section B specifies a particular component mentioned in Section A's general description.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements related to radiation protection design features, while Section B describes specific plant structures and utility systems, with both sections providing information that together helps identify and contextualize the components and supporting infrastructure of the NuScale Power Module (NPM).

Section B describes the NuScale Integral System Test Facility (NIST-1), which replicates and tests the integrated thermal-hydraulic phenomena of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), thereby providing empirical data that supports the design features and regulatory conformance discussed in Section A.

Section B provides specific information about the emergency core cooling system valves, which are components of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) referenced in the analysis question, while Section A discusses regulatory conformance for radiation protection design features of the NPM.

Section B provides specific details about plant features and equipment (such as chiller equipment and instrument air system) that are components or support systems relevant to the NuScale Power Module, whereas Section A only addresses regulatory conformance and does not enumerate components.

Section B describes testing activities that provide technical data and validation for the NPM design referenced in Section A's regulatory conformance table, thereby supporting the claims of compliance in Section A.

Section B describes specific components (ECCS valves, RVVs) that are part of the NuScale Power Module, which is the subject of the analysis question, while Section A only addresses regulatory conformance and does not list or describe components.

Section A lists specific regulatory guidance documents and their conformance status relevant to radiation protection design features, while Section B provides a broader table summarizing conformance with various SRP and DSRS sections, with both sections connecting through their focus on regulatory compliance for the NuScale Power Module's design features.
